Fair Pol-itical Practices Commission
P. O. Box 8 067
Sacramento, cA 95804
Re: Request for Fomal written Adv j.ce
Our Fife: 
50 0-1
centlemen:
As the City Attorney for the City of Glendale, I an hereby
requesting formal written advice pursuant to Government Code
Section 83114(b) and Section 18329 of the Fair Potitical
Practices Cornmission regulations.
I have been expressly authorized by the Honorable Richard
Jutras, Council Member and Member of the Redevelopment Agency
for the City of Glendale, to make this request relatj-ng to his
respons ib i l ities under the Fair pol-itical Practj-ces Act, as
!'/eIl as Eealth and Safety Code Section 33130.
BACKGROUND INFORMATTON
The Honorabl-e Richard Jutras was eLected by the peopfe of the
City of Glendal-e to the position of Council Menber on April 4,
1989. As a member of the city council- of the city of
Glendale, Mr. Jutras sits with other members as a member of
the c]endaLe Redevelopment Agency, a body politic, 
organized
and existing pursuant to Heafth and Safety code Section 33020
et seq.
The Redevelopment Agency of the City of Glendale has created a
proj ect- area in and arounC +-he cen+-raL Busincss Dastr:-c'i- cf
the city of Glendal-e. Pursuant thereto, the Redevelopment
Agency approves pl-ans or policies for the redeveloprnent of the
project area and is ca11ed upon to vote on such projects from
tine to tine.
Recently, Agency Menber Jutras was offered a business
proposition to partici.pate as a 2oA shareholder in a corporate
venture to lease real property within the redevelopment area
to operate a proposed model railroad retail establ j-shrnent.
fhe iroperty to fe leased is within the redevelopment proiect
area-anb hal already been the subject of a project whj-ch was
approved prior to I'Ii. Jutras' etection to the city council and
sirbseque.,t appointment to the Glenda1e Redevelopment Agency'


Fair Political Practices Commission
August 3, L99o
Page Two
The property which wou]d be subject to the lease is now under private
ownership. Additionally, the model, railroad establishment wil-l- be
operated on a day-to-day basis by a non-owner/manager. As a resuft,
Mr. Jutrasr involvement will be strictly 
as a passive investor.
Our office has prepared a l-egal- opinion in which an analysis of the
various applicable code sections, including Hea]th & Safety code
Section 33130 and covernnent Code Sections 87000 et seq. has been
made. We have opined that Agency Member Jutras cannot acquj-re the
interest in reaL property which has been offered pursuant to 33130(c)
of the Health & Safety Code in that the operation of a nodel railroad
establishment is not used in the pursuit of the principat business
occupation or professj-on of Mr. Jutras. The additionaL question has
been posed as to whether such interest could be taken by l4r. Ju+-rasl
spouse or aduft child. 
our review of Heatth & Safety code Section
33130 seems to restrict only the Agency officer fron acquiring any
interest in real property and requj-res that an officer who owns or has
any direct or indirect financial interest in property must disclose
same to the Agency.
REOUEST FOR WRITTEN ADVICE
Your advice is requested with respect to the followj-ng issues:
1. Under Heal-th & Safety Code Section 33130, can Agency Member
Jutrasr adul-t spouse or child acquire a 2ot shareholder's interest in
property rrithin the redevelopment area without subjecting himself to a
potential cl-aim of misconduct in office under Section 33130(a).
2. Under the materiality rules by the FppC, assurnlng that Agency
Member Jutrasr spouse or adult child could acquire an interest in the
aforesaid property pursuant to Health & Safety Code S 33130, what
guidelines would you formulate for us to utilize in determining the
nature and extent of allowable participatj.on by Agency Member Jutras
in voting upon future projects which rnight come before the Agency for
determination.
If i,6s have any questicrts or if j/ou ::eq-.: i i:e additionai inioruration
regarding this request, please do not hesitate to contact me. Because
the offer has recently been made to Mr. Jutras and is of short
duration and, additionally, because the redevelopment project area
activity j-s sornewhat dynarnic, we ltrould sincerely appreciate your most
expeditious attention to this request.
Thank you again for your courtesy and profess iona I ism.
Very truly yours,
city
H. Howard
Attorney

sections.question: 1. Under Health & Safety Code Section 33130, can Agency Member Jutras' adult spouse or child acquire a 20% shareholder's interest in property within the redevelopment area without subjecting himself to a potential claim of misconduct in office under Section 33130(a). 2. Under the materiality rules by the FPPC, assuming that Agency Member Jutras' spouse or adult child could acquire an interest in the aforesaid property pursuant to Health & Safety Code S 33130, what guidelines would you formulate for us to utilize in determining the nature and extent of allowable participation by Agency Member Jutras in voting upon future projects which might come before the Agency for determination.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a City Council member and Redevelopment Agency member may acquire, through his spouse or adult child, a shareholding interest in a business operating within the redevelopment project area, and if so, what voting restrictions or disclosure requirements would apply to the official.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: This is an incoming request from City Attorney to FPPC, not an FPPC response letter. No conclusion section present. Document is dated August 3, 1990, and references Gov't Code Sections 83114(b) and 18329 for formal advice request authority. Background section explains the conflict of interest concern regarding property in the redevelopment area.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: 1. Under Health & Safety Code Section 33130, can Agency Member Jutras' adult spouse or child acquire a 20% shareholder's interest in property within the redevelopment area without subjecting himself to a potential claim of misconduct in office under Section 33130(a). 2. Under the materiality rules by the FPPC, assuming that Agency Member Jutras' spouse or adult child could acquire an interest in the aforesaid property pursuant to Health & Safety Code S 33130, what guidelines would you formulate for us to utilize in determining the nature and extent of allowable participation by Agency Member Jutras in voting upon future projects which might come before the Agency for determination.

embedding.summary: City of Glendale requests formal written FPPC advice regarding whether Redevelopment Agency Member Richard Jutras may indirectly acquire a shareholding interest in a model railroad business within the redevelopment project area through his spouse or adult child, and what voting guidelines would apply.
